$comment: Parity baseline extracted from prototypes/standalone/spike-a (model/journey.json is the source of truth) plus prototypes/e2e/{journey.js, task-list-with-linear-tasks.spec.js, mandatory-fields.spec.js, invalidation.spec.js}. The new obligations spike must match this behaviour in the browser.

pages.title: Give us your email to begin

fields.name: email
fields.label: Email address
fields.validation: Joi string.email (tlds off), required — blank blocks POST with 'Email is required'; bad format 'Enter a valid Email' (model/journey.json:7, validation/compile/field-schemas.js:10-15,81-91)

fields.name: phone
fields.label: UK telephone number
fields.validation: model type 'tel' falls through to textSchema with no pattern — any string accepted (field-schemas.js:67-79)

fields.name: postcode
fields.label: Postcode
fields.validation: pattern-checked only when filled ('Enter a valid Postcode')

fields.name: dateOfBirth
fields.label: Date of birth (day/month/year triple, namePrefix dateOfBirth)
fields.validation: 0 parts filled passes; 1-2 parts or an impossible date blocks with 'Date of birth must be a real date', error keyed dateOfBirth-day (validation/compile/date-field.js:19-37). min-age-17 business rule fires only at CYA submit (journey.json:140-147)

fields.name: registration
fields.label: Registration number
fields.validation: required + pattern ('Registration is required' / 'Enter a valid Registration')

fields.name: year
fields.label: Year of manufacture
fields.validation: integer 1900-2100 when filled ('Year must be a whole number' / 'Year is out of range')

fields.name: vehiclePhoto
fields.label: Upload a photo of your vehicle (optional)
fields.notes: render-only; not in journey.json, never saved

fields.name: hadClaims
fields.label: Have you had any claims in the last 5 years? (radios yes/no)
fields.validation: 'Had claims is required' on blank submit

pages.title: Claims you have added
pages.notes: Manage list: summary rows 'Claim N' with Remove action; buttons 'Add a claim' (or 'Add another claim' once rows exist, secondary, name=action value=add) and 'Continue' (action=continue → markClaimsDone + navNext to cover-type).

fields.name: claimType
fields.label: What type of claim was it?
fields.notes: journey.json item.fields declares claimType required (journey.json:64-74) but the runtime never enforces it — routes/claims.js validates only claimsAddSchema (claimAmount currency); a typeless claim renders as 'Not provided'

fields.name: claimAmount
fields.label: Approximate claim amount
fields.validation: optional; when filled must be whole pounds > 0 ('Claim amount must be a whole number of pounds greater than 0, like 1500') — routes/claims.js:29-34

options.hint: Covers you, your car and other people
Covers other people, plus fire and theft of your car

fields.name: voluntaryExcess
fields.label: Do you want to add a voluntary excess? (radios yes/no)

fields.name: excessAmount
fields.label: Voluntary excess amount
fields.validation: conditional reveal under Yes; when voluntaryExcess=yes and blank, POST blocked with 'Excess amount is required' (validation/compile/index.js:40-48, journey.json:107-111). lte excessAmount<=estimatedValue is a CYA-submit business rule only (journey.json:148-157)

fields.name: extras
fields.label: Add any optional extras (checkboxes)
fields.validation: model marks required (journey.json:118-129); unchecked checkboxes post no key so Joi any.required blocks with 'Extras is required' — at save you must tick at least one. For hub completeness however an answered-but-empty array counts as satisfied (runtime/util.js:23-28)

fields.name: addons
fields.label: What would you like to add? (checkboxes)
fields.notes: 0..N allowed; empty submit saves selectedAddons=[] and redirects to hub (routes/addons/handlers.js:29-34)

fields.name: driverName
fields.label: Full name
fields.notes: not validated at save, but the step only counts complete once truthy (lib/addons/completion.js:4-6)

fields.name: driverDob
fields.label: Date of birth
fields.validation: dobSchema required:false — format checked only when filled

fields.name: relationship
fields.label: What is their relationship to you?
fields.notes: no schema — validatePayload passes any payload through (lib/validate/run.js:29-32)

fields.name: modValue
fields.label: Approximate value added
fields.validation: optional format check; 'Modification value must be a whole number of pounds greater than 0, like 1500' when malformed

fields.name: ncdYears
fields.label: How many years do you want to protect?
fields.validation: integerYearsSchema required:true — blank blocks with 'Enter how many years you want to protect' (lib/addons/catalogue.js:107-114)

pages.title: Your quote
pages.notes: No inputs; button 'Accept and continue' POSTs and redirects to check-your-answers with no state change beyond the premium already persisted on GET.

pages.title: Check your answers
pages.notes: Button 'Accept and get quote'. See cya block.

hub.heading: Get a car insurance quote
hub.progress_line: You have completed {completedCount} of {totalCount} tasks — totalCount is the 3 groups only; add-ons and Get your quote are excluded (journey/hub-view-model.js:89-92, templates/hub.njk:7-9)

rows_in_order.title: Email
rows_in_order.hint: Give us your email to begin

rows_in_order.title: Your driving and cover
rows_in_order.hint: live step titles joined, e.g. 'Driving history, Your claims, Choose your cover, Optional extras' (claims dropped from hint when hadClaims != yes)

rows_in_order.title: one row per selected add-on, in catalogue order (named-driver, modifications, protected-ncd filtered to selection)
rows_in_order.status: 'Completed' plain text or blue 'Incomplete' tag (lib/addons/views.js:31-43); rows appear on selection and disappear when deselected

rows_in_order.title: Get your quote
rows_in_order.link: live ONLY when contract.allComplete(quote); otherwise no href
rows_in_order.status: ready → blue tag 'Not started'; not ready → plain text 'Cannot start yet' with class govuk-task-list__status--cannot-start-yet (journey/hub-view-model.js:47-59)

hub.group_status_derivation: per-step status from the contract, restricted to applicable steps: COMPLETE = every required(-when) field satisfied (steps with no required fields can never be COMPLETE... n/a here, every group step has one); PARTIAL = any field answered; NOT_STARTED otherwise. loop: claimsDone=true → COMPLETE, any claims → PARTIAL, else NOT_STARTED. subtasks: selectedAddons undefined → NOT_STARTED, all selected complete (incl. empty selection) → COMPLETE, else PARTIAL (runtime/selectors/status.js).
hub.group_tag_rendering: all steps COMPLETE → plain text 'Completed' (no tag); any COMPLETE or PARTIAL → tag 'In progress' govuk-tag--light-blue; else tag 'Not started' govuk-tag--grey (hub-view-model.js:33-45)
hub.step_tag_rendering_addons_row: complete → text 'Completed'; not-started → tag 'Not started' govuk-tag--blue; partial → tag 'Incomplete' govuk-tag--blue ('Cannot start yet' branch unreachable for addons) (hub-view-model.js:17-31)
hub.get_your_quote_gating: contract.allComplete = every APPLICABLE step's status === COMPLETE: email, about-you (fullName), your-vehicle (registration), driving-history (hadClaims), claims loop only when hadClaims=yes (claimsDone=true — note 0 claims + Continue still counts COMPLETE on the hub), cover-type (coverType + excessAmount if voluntaryExcess=yes), optional-extras (extras answered), addons (selectedAddons defined and every selected add-on's steps have truthy completion keys). Gating is presentational only — the quote-summary URL itself is not guarded.
hub.task_link_liveness: every group row and add-on row is always a live link regardless of status; only 'Get your quote' toggles between link and plain 'Cannot start yet' text

email_gate.mechanics: NOT a redirect in spike-a. POST {BASE}/start creates a draft (status 'draft', no answers) and redirects straight to the hub (routes/shell.js:29-36). Email is the first hub task — its own single-step group 'Email' (journey/config.js:20). The user opens it like any task; email.email is required at save with a GDS error on blank/invalid.
email_gate.spec_accommodation: The shared specs race two headings after 'Start now' — 'Give us your email to begin' (hand-written prototype redirects pre-hub) vs 'Get a car insurance quote' (spikes land on the hub and click the 'Email' task) — prototypes/e2e/journey.js:151-166. A spike must therefore land on the hub with Email as a clickable task.
email_gate.fulfilment_effect: Saving a valid email sets quote.email, flips the Email step status to complete → the 'Email' group row renders plain-text 'Completed', completedCount increments (1 of 3), and the step counts toward contract.allComplete (so it is one of the preconditions of the 'Get your quote' link). It changes NO journey-level status field — quote.status stays 'draft' until CYA submit. It does not unlock other tasks: every other task row is a live link from the moment the hub first renders.

claims.storage: quote.claims — an array of { claimType, claimAmount } items on the quote record; plus quote.claimsDone boolean (lib/claims.js:21-41)
claims.add: POST {BASE}/{id}/claims/add validates only claimAmount (optional currency format); appends the claim and redirects back to the list. Remove is GET {BASE}/{id}/claims/{index}/remove (filters by index).
claims.list: GET {BASE}/{id}/claims renders summary rows 'Claim N' → '<Type label> — £<amount>' with a Remove action each. POST with action=add → add form; otherwise sets claimsDone=true and continues to cover-type (routes/claims.js:80-86).
claims.entry: The loop only applies when hadClaims === 'yes' (journey.json:59). Saving driving-history with hadClaims=yes routes next → claims list (group navigation over applicable steps).
claims.wipe_mechanics: Flipping hadClaims to 'no' (from driving-history, incl. via CYA ?change=1) runs contract.applyAnswer: it diffs applicableStepIds before/after the merge and clearStep()s any step that stopped applying — for the loop that sets claims=[] and claimsDone=false in the SAME persisted patch (runtime/selectors/mutation.js:38-57). The data is destroyed, not hidden, so a later yes → no → yes round-trip CANNOT rehydrate old claims — the loop returns NOT_STARTED and the list is empty. Verified by prototypes/e2e/invalidation.spec.js (both tests: claim row count 0 after down-flip and after re-flip).
claims.cya_note: hub COMPLETE (claimsDone=true) is possible with 0 claims, but CYA then shows soft prompt / blocks submit with 'Add at least one claim' (validation/assemble/required-errors.js:16-26)

quote.formula: premium = max( round((480 + round(estimatedValue*0.01)) * coverMultiplier) + (hadClaims==='yes' ? 120 : 0) + penaltyPoints*15 - yearsNoClaims*25 + sum(extraCosts), 150 )
quote.persistence: Premium is computed and persisted onto the quote record (quote.premium) on every GET of quote-summary (routes/endings/quote-summary.js:7-10). Store is spike-a's OWN lib/data/quotes.json — a single JSON array file read/written synchronously (lib/store.js); the standalone spike does NOT share prototypes/shared/data/quotes.json (that file belongs to the /prototype tree).
quote.per_journey_isolation: createDraft('spike-a') mints a randomUUID id and tags variant:'spike-a'; every route addresses one quote by {id} path param, so parallel journeys/specs never collide. All lookups are findQuote(id) over the array.
quote.quote_summary_page_shows: h1 'Your quote'; 'Estimated annual premium: £{premium}'; summary list rows Cover (label) and Optional extras (labels joined or 'None'); disclaimer 'This is an illustrative price for a prototype. It is not a real quote.'; button 'Accept and continue'; back link → hub
quote.confirmation_page_shows: govukPanel titled 'Quote confirmed' with 'Your reference number <reference>'; 'We have saved your quote of £{premium} per year.'; 'What happens next — Nothing, this is a prototype…'; link back to /prototype-standalone. reference = 'CI-' + first 6 hex of the uuid, uppercased (lib/quote.js:82-84) — deterministic per quote id.

cya.rows: For every APPLICABLE step in model order, the matching section's rows() from lib/sections/definitions.js: Email; Name / Preferred name / Telephone / Postcode / Country / Date of birth (dd/mm/yyyy or 'Not provided'); Registration / Vehicle (make model year joined) / Estimated value (£n); Years no claims / Recent claims (Yes|No) / Penalty points (default '0'); Claim 1..N ('<type> — £<amount>') or 'Claims: None added' (only when hadClaims=yes); Cover / Voluntary excess (£n or 'None'); Optional extras (labels or 'None'); one row per selected add-on ('Added'|'Incomplete') or 'Added to policy: None'.
cya.change_links: Every row gets a Change action. Plain question steps → {BASE}/{id}/{stepId}?change=1 (GET shows CYA as back link; successful POST redirects straight back to CYA instead of next-in-group — routes/section.js:56-59). Loop (claims) and subtasks (addons) rows link to their own page WITHOUT ?change=1 and return through their own flow (routes/endings/helpers.js:19-22).
cya.soft_prompts_on_load: contract.missingRequired renders a notification banner 'You still need to complete some sections' listing each missing step with a provenance reason (e.g. 'You answered "yes" for Had claims').
cya.submit: POST 'Accept and get quote' runs contract.assembleQuote — hard gate: every applicable required field, 'Add at least one claim' for an empty applicable loop, 'Finish every add-on you selected', plus the two business rules (driver-min-age 17; excessAmount <= estimatedValue when voluntaryExcess=yes). Failures re-render CYA with a GDS error summary linking each error to its change href. Success → updateQuote(status:'quoted', reference:makeReference(id)) → redirect to confirmation (routes/endings/check-your-answers.js:52-81).
cya.post_submit: NOTHING is frozen. status:'quoted' is only ever read by the confirmation guard. Hub, every section page, claims, addons, quote-summary and CYA all remain reachable and editable after submission; re-submitting CYA just re-stamps the same deterministic reference. The three shared specs all END at the 'Quote confirmed' heading (task-list-with-linear-tasks.spec.js:83-84) and never navigate afterwards, so a post-submit freeze would be UNOBSERVED by the specs — but it would still diverge from spike-a's real browser behaviour, which is freely navigable.

routes.notes: chooser page listing the spikes (standalone/index.js) — outside the spike plugin
loop manage list; POST action=add → 302 claims/add, else marks done → 302 next (cover-type)

addon∈{named-driver:[who,relationship], modifications:[describe,value], protected-ncd:[years]}; unknown addon/step → 302 {BASE}/{id}/addons; last step of an addon → 302 hub; step back at index 0 → hub
GET prices + persists premium; POST → 302 check-your-answers
POST ok → 302 confirmation; invalid → re-render with error summary

routes.redirects: every {id}-bearing route redirects to {BASE} when the quote id is unknown (withQuote guards); ?change=1 on section POST redirects to check-your-answers instead of next step; all routes are auth:false

rulings.question: (a) Is post-submit navigation frozen in spike-a or freely navigable?
rulings.answer: Freely navigable. After 'Accept and get quote' sets status:'quoted' + reference, no route checks status except GET confirmation. Hub, all section pages, claims, addons, quote-summary and CYA remain reachable and editable; re-submitting CYA re-runs assembleQuote and re-stamps the same deterministic reference. The shared specs end at the 'Quote confirmed' heading, so a freeze in the new spike would pass the specs unobserved — but it would break browser parity with spike-a, which does not freeze.
rulings.evidence: routes/endings/check-your-answers.js:52-56,73-81 (only status/reference written, nothing gated); routes/endings/confirmation.js:5-7 (the single status check); routes/shell.js:38-56 and routes/section.js:61-82 (no status guards); prototypes/e2e/task-list-with-linear-tasks.spec.js:83-84 (spec ends at 'Quote confirmed')

rulings.question: (b) Is direct-URL access to CYA/later pages guarded or open?
rulings.answer: Open, with exactly two exceptions: (1) every {id} route 302s to the start page when the quote id does not exist (withQuote), and (2) GET confirmation 302s to the start page unless quote.status === 'quoted'. Quote-summary and CYA can be loaded by URL at any point of an incomplete journey — quote-summary happily prices a half-empty quote, CYA shows soft 'you still need to…' prompts, and the hard gate is only on CYA POST. The 'Get your quote' gating is presentational (link withheld), not a route guard.
rulings.evidence: routes/endings/helpers.js:12-15 (withQuote is the only guard); routes/endings/quote-summary.js:24-27 (no completeness check on GET); routes/endings/check-your-answers.js:71 (GET renders regardless); routes/endings/confirmation.js:5-7 (status guard)

rulings.question: (c) How exactly is 'Get your quote' gated?
rulings.answer: The hub row only receives an href (and a blue 'Not started' tag) when contract.allComplete(quote) is true — i.e. every APPLICABLE step's derived status is 'complete': email answered; fullName; registration; hadClaims; the claims loop claimsDone=true when hadClaims=yes (0 claims still counts on the hub); coverType (+ excessAmount when voluntaryExcess=yes); extras answered; selectedAddons defined AND every selected add-on's completion keys truthy. Otherwise the row is plain text 'Cannot start yet' with class govuk-task-list__status--cannot-start-yet and no link. The URL itself is never blocked.
rulings.evidence: journey/hub-view-model.js:47-59 (getYourQuoteItem); runtime/selectors/status.js:57-75 (status + allComplete over applicableStepIds); runtime/selectors/status.js:27-41 (loop/subtasks status)

rulings.question: (d) Are modifications modelled as a collection or fixed fields?
rulings.answer: Fixed fields. 'Declare vehicle modifications' is one add-on with a fixed 2-step linear mini-journey — describe (modDescription textarea, maxlength 200) then value (modValue currency) — whose answers merge into the single object quote.addonData['modifications']. There is no array, no add-another, no per-modification items; completion is simply both keys truthy. The only collection in spike-a is the claims loop.
rulings.evidence: lib/addons/catalogue.js:57-94 (two steps, keys modDescription/modValue); lib/addons/selection.js:11-16 (saveAddonStep merges into addonData[value] object); lib/addons/completion.js:8-12 (every step key truthy)

rulings.question: (e) Does completing Email change any journey-level status?
rulings.answer: No journey-level status changes. Saving a valid email only sets quote.email (quote.status stays 'draft' until CYA submit). Derived effects: the Email step's status becomes 'complete', so its single-step group row renders plain-text 'Completed', completedCount goes 0→1 (of 3), and one precondition of allComplete/'Get your quote' is met. It gates nothing else — all other task rows were already live links.
rulings.evidence: routes/section.js:67-82 (POST just merges collected answers — { email } — via applyAnswer/updateQuote); lib/store.js:39-53 (status set only at createDraft) and routes/endings/check-your-answers.js:52-56 (the only other status write); journey/hub-view-model.js:61-92 (Completed tag + completedCount are derived, not stored)
